description: Zero unit tests exist under src/ — 15+ subpackages (transport
column9: wiring) with ~60 Python files but zero test_*.py files
column13: Create pytest unit/ structure mirroring src/ layout with tests for all core components

description: No docker-compose.yml exists anywhere in the project — test_fabric_integration.py and test_live_protocol_switch.py both require docker compose up -d
proposed_solution: Create docker-compose.yml with FabricService + Redis containers and healthcheck endpoints

description: No pytest.ini pyproject.toml or setup.cfg — no shared markers (unit integration slow) no asyncio_mode default
proposed_solution: Create pytest.ini with asyncio_mode=auto and markers (unit integration slow)

description: No load testing infrastructure — no k6 no Locust no benchmark scripts to verify throughput or saturation points
proposed_solution: Add k6 or Locust scripts covering send/receive/stream scenarios with throughput assertions

description: No tests for failure scenarios — network partition timeout transporter crash session expiry circuit breaker open
proposed_solution: Add fault injection fixtures that simulate TransportError CircuitBreakerOpen SessionExpired and timeout

description: BaseActor exists only in .docs/usage_integration_examples/base_actor_communication.py — not a real package. Import from actors.base_actor fails
proposed_solution: Move BaseActor to src/actors/base_actor.py with __init__.py and fix all import paths

description: src/ directory has no __init__.py — Python cannot import src as a package without PYTHONPATH manipulation
proposed_solution: Add __init__.py to src/ root to make it a proper Python package

description: No requirements.txt or pyproject.toml pins pytest pytest-asyncio httpx jq or any test libraries
proposed_solution: Create requirements-dev.txt or pyproject.toml with all test dependencies pinned

description: No GitHub Actions or any CI configuration — tests never run automatically on push or PR
proposed_solution: Add .github/workflows/test.yml with pytest run on every push and PR

description: No .coveragerc or coverage.py settings — no way to measure test coverage percentage
proposed_solution: Add pytest-cov with coverage config in pyproject.toml and minimum threshold

description: Only one conftest.py exists in .docs/usage_integration_examples/tests/ — no root-level conftest.py for shared fixtures across the project
proposed_solution: Create root conftest.py with shared fixtures (mock_gateway isolated_actor) for the entire test suite
